name: Area-of-Effect Attack
description: An attack that affects multiple targets or positions inside a defined radius, shape, cone, line, or zone.
design_notes: Area attacks create spatial pressure. They need clear telegraphs, target filters, falloff rules, friendly-fire rules, and performance-safe hit queries. The most important design question is whether the area is a warning, a denial zone, a burst payoff, or sustained hazard.
design_purpose: Threaten groups, control space, or punish clustered targets.
Create movement decisions around telegraphs, safe zones, and timing.
Support weapons, spells, hazards, and boss attacks that act on space rather than one target.
player_fantasy: The player feels able to shape the battlefield by affecting a whole area at once.

parameters.name: aoe_shape
parameters.description: Geometric shape used for the area query.

parameters.name: aoe_radius
parameters.description: Primary radius or half-width of the area.

parameters.name: telegraph_duration_ms
parameters.description: Warning window before the area becomes active.

parameters.name: damage_falloff_curve
parameters.description: How damage changes from center to edge.

edge_cases: Targets partly inside the area need center-point, collider-overlap, or coverage rules.
Line of sight and cover should decide whether walls block the effect.
Multiple overlapping areas can stack, refresh, or choose the strongest effect.
Friendly fire, neutral actors, destructibles, and summons need target filters.
common_bugs: Visual radius and damage radius use different scale values.
The area hits targets every frame instead of once per attack instance.
Falloff applies twice because both attack and damage systems reduce the value.
Large AoE queries allocate memory or stall the frame during busy encounters.
balancing_notes: Large radius, short telegraph, and high damage together leave little counterplay.
Persistent zones should be balanced through duration, visibility, stacking, and escape options.
accessibility_notes: Use clear telegraph shapes, high-contrast edges, and non-color cues before damage occurs.
Avoid effects that hide their own safe zones with particles or screen shake.

implementation_notes.unity: Create an aoe_instance_id with shape, radius, target filters, and falloff curve. Query overlaps at activation or tick intervals, track hit target IDs as needed, and apply damage through the resolver.
implementation_notes.godot: Represent the AoE as Area2D/Area3D or explicit physics queries with aoe_instance_id and target filters. Telegraph and damage phases should be separate states with validated timing.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use collision queries, Gameplay Ability target data, or area actors with server-owned aoe_instance_id. Apply LOS, falloff, friendly-fire, and max target rules before damage execution.
implementation_notes.web: Store area attacks as simulation entities with shape, activation time, tick schedule, and hit tracking. Resolve overlap, cover rules, and falloff before emitting combat events.

example_games.title: World of Warcraft
example_games.note: Uses ground telegraphs and area attacks in encounters.

example_games.title: Hades
example_games.note: Uses area attacks for weapons, boons, and enemy pressure.
